raw.title: Proforestation Beyond the Human:  Forests, Climate Emergency, and the Undoing of Mastery
raw.excerpt: In the end, proforestation offers us a choice about who we wish to become. We can continue to treat forests as instruments to repair a damaged atmosphere, pulling them into our orbit as another tool in a human-centred project. Or we can accept their invitation to live differently: to slow down, to restrain ourselves, to share space and time with other beings whose existence does not revolve around us.

llm_processed.summary: This article argues for proforestation as a way to let forests develop naturally to combat the climate crisis, challenging human-centered views of forests as mere carbon sinks.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: Connects to agroecology by emphasizing natural ecosystem complexity over human intervention.
